answer:
**Yājñavalkya Smṛti 2.21** (in Chapter 2, "On Legal Procedure") does not concern witnesses.[^1] It states a rule for resolving conflicts between scriptural sources: "In a conflict between two Smṛtis, reason based on custom is stronger in legal matters. But it is the established rule that Dharmaśāstra is more powerful than Arthaśāstra."[^1]

Witnesses (*sākṣin*) are instead addressed as one of three primary proofs (*pramāṇa*)—"document, possession, and witnesses"—immediately afterward at 2.22, with resort to ordeal (*divya*) if none are available.[^2] Detailed rules appear in section 5 (verses 68–83), emphasizing competent witnesses, their examination, and penalties.[^3]

### Competent Witnesses (2.68–72)
- Ideal: ascetics, the charitable, good-family men, truth-tellers, those prioritizing *dharma*, upright men, fathers, and the wealthy.[^4]
- Minimum: three, versed in Śruti/Smṛti, matching the parties' caste/*varṇa* (or any *varṇa* for any).[^5]
- Incompetent: women, children, elders, gamblers, drunkards, insane, grave sinners, actors, heretics, forgers, sensory defectives, outcastes, friends/enemies with interest, thieves, violent criminals, convicts, exiles.[^6][^7]
- Exceptions: A single approved *dharma*-knowing witness suffices; anyone may testify in adultery, theft, assault, violence.[^8]

### Examination and Testimony (2.73–83)
- Judge admonishes witnesses before parties: False testimony yields hells of sinners/arsonists/child-killers, forfeits past-life merit.[^9]
- Non-testimony (knowing a debt): Fined full amount + 10% after 46 days; equals false witness in sin/punishment.[^10]
- Conflicts: Majority rules; if equal, more virtuous; if virtuous equal, most virtuous.[^11] Victory to supported claim; later/better/more numerous testimony overrides prior.[^12]
- Penalties: False witnesses fined double dispute amount (Brāhmaṇa banished); recanting testimony: 8x fine (Brāhmaṇa banished). Exception: false testimony to avert certain death (purified by Sarasvatī rite for twice-born).[^13]

[^1]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [para. p-048b9a](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-048b9a).
[^2]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [para. p-e6e133](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-e6e133).
[^3]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [paras. p-5c0bb3 to p-ff1854](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-5c0bb3).
[^4]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [para. p-5c0bb3](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-5c0bb3).
[^5]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [para. p-18320c](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-18320c).
[^6]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [para. p-478abd](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-478abd).
[^7]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [para. p-f5049f](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-f5049f).
[^8]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [para. p-33a082](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-33a082).
[^9]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [paras. p-574029 to p-2034fe](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-574029).
[^10]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [paras. p-16fa67 to p-908432](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-16fa67).
[^11]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [para. p-b221f9](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-b221f9).
[^12]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [paras. p-f02be5 to p-fbf5a7](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-f02be5).
[^13]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [paras. p-dbd716 to p-5cd0fa](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-dbd716).
